blocks.text:
A synthetic fixture whose only purpose is coverage: each figure below stresses a family of
commands that the example bank does not otherwise reach.

## Fields, charges and flux

blocks.source:
charge q1 at=(-1,0) sign=+ label="q_1"
charge q2 at=(1,0) sign=- label="q_2"
dipole at=(0,-2.4) sep=1 angle=30 moment=true label="\vec{p}"
fieldline from=(-1,0) to=(1,0) bend=0.5
streamline from=(-1,0) to=(1,0) bend=-0.5
vectorfield at=(-3.5,0.8) width=2 height=2 n=3 dir=out
equipotential at=(0,0) r=1.6
gaussian at=(0,0) r=2.1 label="S"

blocks.text: ## Mechanics: pivots, rods, pulleys and coils

blocks.source:
pivot at=(0,3) label="O"
hinge at=(3,3)
rod from=(0,3) to=(2,1.5) label="\ell"
pulley at=(-2,3) r=0.4
pendulum P at=(3,3) length=2 angle=25 value="\theta" label="m"
spring from=(-3.4,0) to=(-3.4,2) coils=5 label="k"
coil at=(0,-2) turns=2 dr=0.2
helix at=(2,-2) turns=2 dr=0.2
block M at=(-1,-1) size=0.7 label="M"
rope from=(-2,3) to=(-2,0.2)
string from=(2,0) to=(2,2)

lens at=(0,0) height=2.4 type=convex
lens at=(3.2,0) height=2.4 type=concave
ray from=(-3,0.5) to=(0,0.3)
ray from=(0,0.3) to=(3.2,-0.2) arrow=false
wavefront at=(-3,0) n=3 r0=0.4 dr=0.4
wavefront at=(3.2,0) n=2 r0=0.3 dr=0.3 from=200 to=340
arc at=(0,-2.2) r=1 from=0 to=135
spiral at=(0,2.2) turns=3 dr=0.25 a0=90 label="\phi"

axes3d at=(-2.4,-1) size=2.2
sphere at=(2.2,0) r=1 label="R"
flame at=(-2.4,-2.6) size=0.6
zigzag from=(-1,2.4) to=(2,2.4) amplitude=0.2 periods=5 label="cut"
current from=(-2.4,3) to=(1,3)
segment from=(-2.4,-3.4) to=(2.2,-3.4)
arrow from=(0,-3.4) to=(0,-1.4) label="\vec{v}"
text at=(0,1) text="note"

meta.solution:
This file is not a real problem. It exists so the conformance corpus exercises every figure
command (and every alias) that the curated examples happen not to use, giving the JS port a
byte-identical SVG parity target for the whole DSL rather than just the common subset.
meta.subject: physics
meta.title: DSL command coverage (conformance fixture)
